title: Botrychium × longistitatum
intro: Botrychium × longistitatum is a sterile, hybrid species of the genus Botrychium, commonly known as rattlesnake fern or lady fern, characterized by its unique, fern-like appearance. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it potentially useful in the treatment of various diseases and conditions. The bioactive constituents of Botrychium × longistitatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Botrychium × longistitatum is a sterile hybrid between Botrychium lanceolatum and Botrychium simplex, both of which are species of the genus Botrychium, commonly known as lady ferns. This hybrid is part of the family Ophioglossaceae, which is a group of ferns known for their unique, often strap-like or tongue-shaped fronds. The classification of Botrychium × longistitatum is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Equisetophyta, which includes vascular plants. The classification places Botrychium × longistitatum within the class Polypodiopsida and the order Ophioglossales. The specific classification of Botrychium × longistitatum reflects its relationship to other ferns in the family Ophioglossaceae.
uses: Botrychium × longistitatum, also known as the long-stalked rattlesnake fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's fronds and rhizomes are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal value. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The rhizomes are also believed to have astringent properties, which may aid in the treatment of diarrhea and other gastrointestinal issues. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

constituents: Botrychium × longistitatum contains various active constituents, including fernan and longistitiol, which have been identified as its key bioactive compounds. Fernan has been shown to possess significant anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Longistitiol, on the other hand, has been found to exhibit antiviral and antioxidant activities. Research suggests that these compounds play a crucial role in the plant's medicinal properties. Further studies are needed to fully understand the extent of its bioactive potential.
